name: implement-two-tier-shot-20260406-1054
idea:
Implement two-tier shot groups on the video editor timeline: suggested (inferred, dashed border) and pinned (persistent, solid border).

## Core concept
The current useShotGroups inference becomes 'suggested' groups shown with dashed borders. Users can promote them to 'pinned' groups which are stored in timeline config, persist across refresh, and enable advanced features.

## Data model
PinnedShotGroup stored in timelineConfig:
- shotId, trackId, clipIds[], mode: 'images' | 'video', videoAssetKey?, imageClipSnapshot? (for swap-back)

## When pinned groups are created (explicit + implicit):

### Explicit:
1. Right-click a suggested group → 'Pin as Shot Group' - writes PinnedShotGroup to config
2. Drop a shot from shot list/gallery onto timeline - creates pinned group with images expanded as clips

### Implicit (auto-pin):
3. 'Switch to Final Video' on a suggested group - auto-pins first (snapshots image clips), then swaps to video mode
4. When an image is added to a shot that already has a pinned group on the timeline - auto-inserts the new image clip into the pinned group's track at the right position

## Visual distinction:
- Suggested: dashed border, lighter color (current border style → dashed)
- Pinned: solid border, full color (current styling), shot name visible

## Pinned group behavior:
- Clips drag together as a unit (grouped drag)
- Image ↔ video toggle: switch stores snapshot of image clips, restores on switch-back. Single applyEdit each direction.
- Shot image sync: when images are added/removed from a shot, pinned group auto-updates (subscribe to shot changes via realtime or ShotsContext)
- Context menu: Pin/Unpin, Switch to Video/Images, Jump to Shot, Generate Video

## Key architectural decisions:
- pinnedShotGroups stored in TimelineData (persisted via timeline config, undoable)
- useShotGroups still runs for inference but pinned groups take priority (suppress inferred for same shot+track)
- PinnedShotGroup clipIds are the source of truth for which clips belong to the group
- When a pinned group exists, the shot group overlay reads from the pinned data, not from inference

## Key files to research:
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts - current inference
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts - current swap (to be replaced)
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts - applyEdit, TimelineEditMutation, timeline config persistence
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts - drag behavior (need grouped drag)
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts - drop from outside (need shot drop)
- src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts - TimelineData type, ClipMeta
- src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts - AssetRegistry types
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx - rendering shot groups
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ShotGroupContextMenu.tsx - context menu
- src/shared/contexts/ShotsContext.tsx - shot data, realtime updates
- src/shared/hooks/shots/useShotImages.ts - shot image data
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts - asset registration
- src/tools/video-editor/contexts/TimelineEditorContext.ts - editor context/providers

history.message: Inserted new step after S12: Phase 6 Shot Drop: (A) Add new shot drag type to shared DnD in src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts with setShotDropData and getShotDropData. (B) Add drag source in shot list components. (C) Update onTimelineDragOver in useExternalDrop.ts to accept shot type. (D) Extract pure clip-builder helper from handleAssetDrop for atomic multi-clip creation. (E) Add handleShotDrop: for each shot image build asset + clip + sequential timing, create PinnedShotGroup with mode:images. (F) Single applyEdit for atomic creation.

Inserted new step after S13: Phase 7 Auto-Sync: Create usePinnedGroupSync hook (or add to usePinnedShotGroups). Subscribe to ShotsContext changes for shots that have pinned groups on the timeline. When images are added to a shot with a pinned group in mode:images: register new image assets, create clips with duration matching average of existing group clips, insert at correct position in track, update PinnedShotGroup clipIds. When images are removed: remove corresponding clips, update clipIds. Single applyEdit per sync. Guard: only sync when mode===images. Use realtime shot events for fast detection.

overrides.note:
Addressing critique flags:

FLAG-001 (suppression granularity): Use clipIds-based suppression instead of shotId:trackId. A pinned group stores its clipIds. When filtering inferred groups, suppress any inferred group whose clipIds are a subset of a pinned group's clipIds. This handles multiple clusters of the same shot on one track.

FLAG-002/correctness-2/callers-1 (applyEdit config mutations): Add a new mutation type to TimelineEditMutation: { type: 'pinnedShotGroups', pinnedShotGroups: PinnedShotGroup[] }. This is cleaner than trying to squeeze metadata into the existing config or rows paths. The commit handler reads current data, replaces pinnedShotGroups, and rebuilds. For switch-to-video which needs BOTH rows changes AND pinnedShotGroups changes, use the existing type:'rows' mutation but add an optional pinnedShotGroupsOverride field.

FLAG-003 (grouped drag): Defer group expansion to after drag threshold is crossed, not on pointer-down. On pointer-down, just record that the clip is in a pinned group. On first pointermove past threshold, expand draggedClipIds to include all group clips.

FLAG-004/scope-1/all_locations-3 (shot drop DnD): Add a new drag type 'shot' to shared DnD utilities. Add drag source in shot list components. Add acceptance in onTimelineDragOver. This is Phase 6 and can be a follow-up if needed.

FLAG-005/all_locations-1 (migrateToFlatTracks): Explicitly update migrateToFlatTracks to preserve pinnedShotGroups. It currently rebuilds config with an allowlist - add pinnedShotGroups to that list.

all_locations-2 (rowsToConfig callers): Update render-bounds.validation.test.ts and any other direct callers.

issue_hints-1 (clip lifecycle): When a clip in a pinned group is manually deleted, remove it from the group's clipIds. If clipIds becomes empty, remove the pinned group. If in video mode and the video clip is deleted, auto-switch back to images from snapshot if available, otherwise remove the group.

issue_hints-2 (hasFinalVideo guard): Preserve the existing guard - Switch to Video only shows when finalVideoMap.get(shotId) exists. Switch to Images only shows when mode is video.

conventions-1 (reuse insertion helpers): Reuse useAssetManagement helpers for shot drop clip creation rather than hand-building.

conventions-2 (mutation model): All pinned group changes go through applyEdit, never direct mutation of dataRef.current. This preserves undo/history.

overrides.reason: Remaining flags are valid refinements, addressed as follows: (1) Cross-track trackId updates: will update pinnedShotGroups.trackId in the same applyEdit that moves clips across tracks. (2) Reconcile-on-delete double history: will use a useEffect that patches pinnedShotGroups as part of the NEXT edit rather than a separate reconcile edit - stale clipIds in pinnedShotGroups are harmless until the next real edit since the overlay already handles missing clips gracefully. (3) Phases 6-7 deferred: user approved incremental delivery - pin/toggle/grouped-drag ships first, drop and auto-sync follow. (4) Test coverage: will add tests for the new mutation path and save-utils during execution. (5) stableSignature: the new mutation type will go through the full materializeData rebuild, not post-hoc patching. (6) Shot drop helper extraction: will extract a pure clip-builder from handleAssetDrop for atomic multi-clip creation when Phase 6 is implemented.

Phase 6 and 7 were added back per user request. The critique flags from v2 were already addressed in the revision notes. New flags for Phase 6/7 are implementation details that will be handled during execution: (1) shot drag type needs shared DnD plumbing - will implement. (2) clip-builder extraction from handleAssetDrop - will extract pure helper. (3) auto-sync needs debouncing to avoid rapid-fire edits - will debounce. (4) cross-track trackId updates already covered in T12. All other flags are carry-overs from the already-addressed v2 critique.
